tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, low serum bicarbonate, and significant proteinuria with glycosuria and mild ketonuria, suggesting impaired renal function, likely chronic kidney disease with possible tubular dysfunction. Urine is cloudy, brown, and has low specific gravity, further supporting renal impairment. No evidence of urinary tract infection. Recommend nephrology evaluation and further assessment for underlying etiology and glycemic control.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
